D9 BiologyL6 CreateReference

DsRed → mCherry — Engineering a Monomer

native DsRed is a tetramer (bad for fluorescent fusions); Shaner 2004 directed-evolved it through dimers to monomeric mCherry — a single-spot labelling protein

DsRed (from Discosoma coral) was the first cloned RFP — but it's an obligate tetramer, making fluorescent-fusion experiments unreliable. Shaner et al. 2004 used directed evolution: mutate interface residues, screen for monomers that retain folding, iterate. Result: mCherry, monomeric and brighter, the standard RFP today.

Toggle DsRed/mFruit forms; brightness and oligomeric state change.
